title: Login flow — request / response samples
description: Illustrative wire shapes for the four login flow branches: (1) plain success — no MFA enrolled; (2) MFA challenge issued; (3) MFA verified — session issued; (4) failed. Every credential-sensitive field is shown as-if-populated but callers should treat these values as opaque bearers. Real production requests use real secrets; these are shape references only.

flow_1_plain_success.description: Login without MFA enrolled. LoginAction returns 200 with LoginResult immediately.

flow_2_mfa_challenge_issued.description: Login when MFA is enrolled. LoginAction verifies password, then returns 202 with MfaChallengeResult instead of a session.

flow_3_mfa_verified_session_issued.description: Followup to flow 2 — client submits TOTP code, receives session.

flow_4_failed.description: Wrong password. Enumeration-safe — same response for unknown email, wrong password, disabled identity, unverified email.

flow_5_locked.description: Account is locked (identity.check_lockout middleware refused). Response includes unlock_url for the reset flow.

body.message: Account is locked. Reset your password to unlock.

flow_6_rate_limited.description: Too many attempts. Retry-After header set.

body.message: Too many login attempts. Try again later.

invariants: Every enumeration-safe path (AUTH_FAILED) returns the SAME body shape regardless of whether the email is unknown, the password wrong, or the identity soft-deleted / disabled. The response CANNOT be distinguished by shape / status / timing.

Constant-time login: LoginAction's duration histogram (stackra.auth.login_duration_ms) shows unknown_email + password_mismatch + soft_deleted all fall in the same P95 bucket range. The check_lockout middleware runs BEFORE password comparison to preserve this property.
The plaintext token + refresh_token are shown ONCE — subsequent responses (refresh, session-list) never return the plaintexts again.
